spectrumScore.rounding: half-up via EXACT INTEGER ARITHMETIC (mandatory). IEEE-float round() and floor(x+0.5) are non-conforming: raw 33 yields 57 instead of the correct 58 in double precision, and banker's rounding mis-rounds every .5 boundary. Verified defect class in the v1.0 audit.
spectrumScore.latticeNote: Attainable spectrum values step by 2.5 raw-point equivalents (…, 50, 53, 55, 58, 60, …); a score2-score3 gap of exactly 11 is mathematically unreachable, so the <=10 third-dominant rule operationally brackets 10 (include) vs 12 (exclude).

facetScores.compute: Per scale, mean coded value of ACT, ENV, and NRG items separately (facet assignment in items.json).

interestEnergyDivergence.rule: Flag any scale where |mean(NRG coded) - mean(ACT coded)| >= 1.5
interestEnergyDivergence.meaning: Possible state confound (burnout, illness, mood) suppressing or inflating the energy facet independent of interest. Report note + retest advice; do not silently deliver the scale score as pure interest.
interestEnergyDivergence.evidence: v1.0 audit personas P75, P88 (illness manufactured false differentiation)

dominantAvatarRule.rank2: Dominant if its Spectrum Score >= 40; otherwise report a Solo Peak profile (single-avatar narrative, no combination card, exploration guidance)
dominantAvatarRule.soloPeakFlag: if score1 - score2 >= 25, lead the report with the rank-1 narrative; present the combination as secondary

order: 2. higher ACT-facet sum (activity preference is the most interest-central facet)
3. if still tied at a pair-defining or third-slot boundary: present BOTH candidate readings explicitly as 'twin readings' — never silently pick one
tieBreak.note: v1.0 had no tie rules; ties were resolved by implementation sort order and occurred in ~30% of simulated realistic profiles (audit Batches C, D, F, K, L).

dominantAvatarRule.combinationLookup: Normalize the dominant pair to canonicalAvatarOrder before lookup. v1.0 tool bug: alphabetical sort broke the lookup for 7 of 15 pairs.

resultPrecedence: 1. VALIDITY: verdict 'Potentially invalid' -> suppress Dominant Avatars, combination, and career families entirely; deliver only the validity explanation and re-take/consultant-review guidance.
2. FLAT PROFILE: Differentiation Index < 15 (on a valid protocol) -> deliver the breadth narrative (Section 6.6 flat-profile language); do NOT deliver a combination profile.
3. LOW ELEVATION: top Spectrum Score < 50 -> deliver combination as tentative with exploration-first guidance.
4. NORMAL: deliver combination profile per dominantAvatarRule.

confidence.basis: margin = score2 - score3 (for the third-slot decision) and score1 - score2 (for pair ordering); projected SEM ~= 5 spectrum points

bands.label: Low
bands.reportLanguage: adjacent Avatars are statistically interchangeable — explore both readings

differentiationIndex.caveat: DI measures range, not top-of-profile separation; a single low scale can certify a weak profile as well-differentiated. Consultants should also inspect score1 - score3.

cautionLevel: long-string: max identical consecutive run >= 12 OR modal response >= 80% of all 65 responses
acquiescence: mean RAW response across the 60 scored items >= 4.3
elevation: all six Spectrum Scores >= 70 AND Differentiation Index < 20
intra-scale coherence: on >= 3 scales, |mean(positively keyed coded) - mean(reverse keyed coded)| >= 2 (possible misreading of reversed statements)
noteOnly: single consistency pair |difference| == 2 (explicit v1.1 ruling: a lone mild pair is a NOTE, not a caution — v1.0 left this undefined and it flipped verdicts, audit personas P66/P69)
extreme response style: endpoints (1 or 5) >= 85% of responses (style annotation, never a validity signal — v1.0's Q17 'Agree' target false-flagged legitimate extreme responders)
flat profile (handled by resultPrecedence, not by the validity verdict)

verdicts.valid: no invalid-level and no caution-level signals
verdicts.potentiallyInvalid: any invalid-level signal, OR two or more caution-level signals

validity.suppressionRule: 'Potentially invalid' MUST suppress dominant/combination output in the data layer, not only in report prose (v1.0 audit: an every-flag protocol was still named 'The Creative Director').
documentedLimitations: The battery detects INATTENTION and response-style anomalies. It does NOT detect motivated distortion: targeted single-scale faking is undetectable by design (audit P76) — one reason selection/hiring use is prohibited.
Attention checks and consistency pairs are non-redundant (each catches failure classes the other misses; audit P72 vs P73) — both are mandatory.
Q1-Q20 contains no reverse-keyed item, so long-string screening is structurally weaker in the first third; v1.2 item pool should interleave earlier reversals.
